utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: morning christine , nice to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to see you too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i know my nurse told you a little bit of- ... um , about dax and i'm gon na tell dax a little bit about you , if that's okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: go for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . um , uh , this is christine . she's a 63-year-old female patient . you're here for your routine , um , yearly exam . um , i know you have a history of high blood pressure and you've had some depression in the past . and i also know you did have a carpal tunnel release done about two years ago . um , how have you been doing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing well . um , the depression standpoint is doing great . i've been using , um , the medication you prescribed me last time and going to therapy every week . and that's really helped me manage .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you're sleeping well at night ? and everything's been going well from that standpoint ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i've been getting a full eight hours .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no thoughts of hurting yourself or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , good . um , and , um , i- i know you've had some high blood pressure in the past . have you been checking your blood pressure ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have . it's been a little bit up and down-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: lately , i have n't had a normal , um , normal reading- reading in a couple of weeks . um , it's either been high or low . um , has n't really stayed put .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: has n't really stayed put ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , you've been talking your medication though , right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i have .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my diet's been a little all over the place . i have been really stressed with work and putting in more hours, so you know, it has been hard. i have not had time to go to the grocery store, let alone prepare and cook meals. so, i have been eating a lot of frozen meals.

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that type of thing .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and have you had any headaches or swelling or anything like that that's been abnormal ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have some headaches but i just thought that was looking at a computer screen all day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well , that's fair .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , exactly .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay good . and , um ... i know my nurse did a review of systems . other than what we've just talked about , has there been anything else that you wanted to add to that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , my carpal tunnel release has been doing well . on , uh , we did that , i think it was two years ago now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , on my right , um , wrist , and that's been great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great , great . no numbness or tingling in your hands ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: fingers ? okay , good . all right , well , um , lem me do a quick exam if that's okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: deep breath . squeeze my fingers for me . feel me touch you here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: can you touch down here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: can you push your leg out ? push this one up . pull it back . yeah , good . okay . so on my exam , um ... your exam's essentially normal , your lungs are clear , are- ... you do n't have any adenopathy in your neck , you do n't have any , um , uh , hyperthyroidism . um , your heart is regular without any murmurs . um , your grips are equal , neurologically intact , strength is good , pulses are good . so overall , um , your exam is fine . let's take a- a look at some of your labs and vital signs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , show me the blood pressure . okay , so here in the office today it is a little bit elevated , okay ? so your blood pressure is a little bit high . so the results , um , do show that . um , hey dragon , show me the labs . okay , so good news is is your- your labs are all essentially normal , so the results of your labs are essentially normal , okay ? so ... um , my assessment is that your- ... you do have hypertension still . um , and i think we need to increase your blood pressure medicine a little bit , okay ? so i'm gon na increase your lisinopril to 10 milligrams a day , just once a day , um , but i'm gon na increase that a little bit , okay ? and then want you to check it and i'm gon na have you come back in a month and we'll see how you're doing with regards to that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , your second problem is your dep- depression , you're doing great , i do n't wan na change anything at this point . let's just stay- ... we'll change one thing with your high blood pressure-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i do n't wan na change any other medications at this point in time , so let's just leave that as it is .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then your third problem was your carpal tunnel . that seems to be doing well . if you notice any tingling or any changes or you get some of those symptoms back , just let me know . okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . anything else i can do for you today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . uh , lem me get you checked out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the report .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 63-year-old female presenting for an annual exam with a history of hypertension, depression, and a right carpal tunnel release two years ago.
Depression
The patient reports managing depression well with medication and weekly therapy.
The doctor asks if the patient is sleeping well.
The patient reports sleeping eight hours per night.
The doctor asks if the patient has thoughts of self-harm.
The patient denies thoughts of self-harm.
Hypertension
The doctor asks if the patient has been monitoring her blood pressure.
The patient reports checking her blood pressure at home.
The patient reports fluctuating blood pressure readings over the last two weeks.
The doctor asks if the patient is taking her blood pressure medication.
The patient confirms she is taking her blood pressure medication.
The patient reports poor diet due to increased work stress and hours.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced headaches or swelling.
The patient reports headaches, attributing them to increased screen time.
Carpal tunnel
The patient reports her right carpal tunnel release from two years ago is doing well.
The doctor asks if the patient has numbness or tingling in her hands or fingers.
The patient denies numbness or tingling in her hands or fingers.
The doctor declares intent to perform a physical exam.
Physical exam findings: lungs clear, no neck adenopathy, no hyperthyroidism, heart regular without murmurs, grips equal, neurologically intact, strength and pulses good.
Office blood pressure is elevated; laboratory results are normal.
Increase lisinopril to 10 mg once daily.
Patient to monitor blood pressure at home and return in one month.
The doctor decides to maintain the current depression treatment regimen.
The patient is instructed to contact the office if carpal tunnel symptoms return.
